description: Charcoal and blackbird feathers are 'nuko'. Their color remains black in strong light. 'nuelo' (dark) instead reports low illumination; before another color, it marks a dark shade.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip at the upper teeth for dental nasal 'n' and carry the voice through the nose into rounded high-back 'u'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k', then release into rounded mid-back 'o'. Stress falls on 'nu'.
usage_notes: 'nuko kerou' is a black stone. The registered compound 'nuko pelori' names a blackbird through the same ordinary adjective pattern.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: the achromatic category at the low end of perceived lightness
